# Interface Contracts: Plugin Clay

**Purpose**: Define exact function signatures for wheel library changes and key data structures shared across skills. Markdown skills (SKILL.md) don't export functions — their interface is defined by their YAML frontmatter and input/output conventions. This file covers the Bash library functions and JSON schemas.

**Generated by**: `/plan` (Phase 1: Design & Contracts)
**Consumed by**: `/implement` (all tasks must match these signatures)

## Rules

- Every exported function in `plugin-wheel/lib/workflow.sh` MUST match the signature defined here
- JSON schemas MUST be followed exactly — no extra required fields, no missing fields
- SKILL.md frontmatter format MUST match the conventions shown here
- If a signature needs to change during implementation, update this file FIRST

## Module: plugin-wheel/lib/workflow.sh

### Existing function (no changes)

```bash
# FR-012: Load and validate a workflow JSON file
# Params: $1 = workflow file path
# Output (stdout): validated workflow JSON
# Exit: 0 on valid, 1 on invalid (with error message on stderr)
workflow_load() { ... }  # UNCHANGED
```

### New function

```bash
# FR-029: Discover workflows declared in installed plugin manifests
# Params: none
# Output (stdout): JSON array of plugin workflow descriptors
# Exit: 0 on success (empty array if no plugins found), 1 on parse error
# Output format: [{"name": "clay-sync", "plugin": "clay", "path": "/abs/path/to/clay-sync.json", "readonly": true}, ...]
workflow_discover_plugin_workflows()
```

## Module: plugin-clay/.claude-plugin/plugin.json

### Schema

```json
{
  "name": "clay",
  "version": "000.000.000.000",
  "description": "Idea-to-project pipeline for Claude Code. Market research, naming, PRD creation, and repo scaffolding.",
  "author": { "name": "yoshisada" },
  "homepage": "https://github.com/yoshisada/ai-repo-template",
  "dependencies": ["wheel", "shelf"],
  "workflows": ["workflows/clay-sync.json"]
}
```

**Fields**:
- `name` (string, required): Plugin identifier — used in `<plugin>:<workflow-name>` syntax
- `version` (string, required): Semver-style version matching repo VERSION
- `description` (string, required): One-line plugin description
- `author` (object, required): `{ "name": string }`
- `homepage` (string, optional): Repository URL
- `dependencies` (string[], optional): FR-002 — list of required plugin names
- `workflows` (string[], optional): FR-028 — array of workflow file paths relative to plugin root

## Module: plugin-clay/workflows/clay-sync.json

### Schema

```json
{
  "name": "clay-sync",
  "description": "Sync product ideas and PRDs from products/ to Obsidian vault",
  "steps": [
    {
      "id": "scan-products",
      "type": "command",
      "description": "Scan products/ directory and derive product statuses",
      "command": "bash -c '...'",
      "output_path": ".clay/sync-manifest.json"
    },
    {
      "id": "sync-to-obsidian",
      "type": "agent",
      "description": "Create/update Obsidian notes for each product using shelf MCP",
      "prompt": "...",
      "context_from": ["scan-products"]
    },
    {
      "id": "sync-research",
      "type": "agent",
      "description": "Sync research findings as linked notes in Obsidian",
      "prompt": "...",
      "context_from": ["scan-products"]
    }
  ]
}
```

## Module: SKILL.md Frontmatter Convention

All clay skills follow this frontmatter pattern:

```yaml
---
name: <skill-name>
description: <one-line description for skill discovery>
---
```

### Skill Input/Output Conventions

| Skill | Input | Output | FR |
|-------|-------|--------|-----|
| idea-research | `$ARGUMENTS` (1-5 sentence idea) | `products/<slug>/research.md` | FR-004-009 |
| project-naming | `$ARGUMENTS` (product context or slug) | `products/<slug>/naming.md` | FR-010-014 |
| create-prd | `$ARGUMENTS` (idea or product slug) | `products/<slug>/PRD.md`, `PRD-MVP.md`, `PRD-Phases.md` | FR-015-021 |
| create-repo | `$ARGUMENTS` (product slug or PRD path) | GitHub repo + local clone | FR-022-027 |
| clay-list | none | stdout table | FR-036-037 |

### Product Status Derivation (shared across clay-list and clay-sync)

```bash
# Derive product status from artifact presence
# Params: $1 = product directory path (e.g., products/my-product)
# Output (stdout): one of: idea, researched, named, prd-created, repo-created
# Logic:
#   - .repo-url file exists           → "repo-created"
#   - PRD.md + PRD-MVP.md + PRD-Phases.md exist → "prd-created"
#   - naming.md exists                → "named"
#   - research.md exists              → "researched"
#   - directory exists but empty      → "idea"
clay_derive_status()
```

This function is inlined in each SKILL.md that needs it (clay-list, clay-sync workflow) — not a shared library. Keep the logic identical in both places.

## Module: plugin-wheel/skills/wheel-run/SKILL.md

### Updated Resolution Logic (FR-031)

Step 1 of wheel-run MUST be updated to resolve `<plugin>:<workflow-name>` syntax:

```bash
# Resolve workflow file from name or plugin:name syntax
# Input: WORKFLOW_NAME (may contain ":" for plugin reference)
# Output: WORKFLOW_FILE path
if [[ "$WORKFLOW_NAME" == *":"* ]]; then
  PLUGIN_NAME="${WORKFLOW_NAME%%:*}"
  WF_NAME="${WORKFLOW_NAME#*:}"
  # Look up plugin workflow via workflow_discover_plugin_workflows
  # Find entry where plugin == PLUGIN_NAME and name == WF_NAME
  # Set WORKFLOW_FILE to entry.path
else
  WORKFLOW_FILE="workflows/${WORKFLOW_NAME}.json"
fi
```

## Module: plugin-wheel/skills/wheel-list/SKILL.md

### Updated Discovery Logic (FR-029)

After scanning `workflows/` directory, wheel-list MUST also call `workflow_discover_plugin_workflows()` and display plugin workflows in a separate section labeled "Plugin Workflows (read-only)".
